raw_segments: The video opens with a close-up of a warrior wearing a Medieval Helmet with a chainmail coif, his face partially obscured, gazing intently into the distance under a hazy, overcast sky, as a male narrator describes William as "aged, immensely stout, and in deteriorating health," crossing to the Duchy to meet a new threat. The scene abruptly shifts to a chaotic Battle Scene, with intense flames engulfing the frame and smoke billowing, visually representing the narrator's account of "William's troops storm[ing] the city of Nantes and put[ting] it to the torch." The narrator continues, detailing how William, "riding exultantly through the burning streets," was either struck by a seizure or thrown from his stumbling mount, suffering a "fatal internal injury." The camera then tilts upward to reveal a towering Ruined Castle, its weathered stone walls reaching into a cloudy sky, emphasizing its imposing, historical presence. This is followed by a bright, starburst Sun rising over a dark treeline, casting a dramatic, almost divine light. The narrative transitions to a sepia-toned Historical Sketch of a Motte-and-Bailey Castle, depicting a ceremonial scene with a crowned figure and a woman, with Latin inscriptions, as the narrator explains the dying king "began settling his affairs," confirming his errant son Robert in his long for Normandy and his second son, William Rufus, as the future King of England. The final shot shows a group of Armored Warriors standing in formation on a grassy field, one holding a yellow flag with a green serpent emblem and another a black flag with a white bird, their armor detailed and their expressions resolute, symbolizing military unity and command.

The segment opens with a live reenactment of armored warriors, their chainmail glinting on a windswept beach, as a male narrator recounts how 'William' rose from obscurity to conquer the richest land in Western Europe. One warrior removes his helmet, revealing a determined face, while others hold heraldic flags—a black one with a white hand and flame, and a yellow one with a green serpent—symbolizing factional identity. The scene transitions to a close-up of the Bayeux Tapestry, depicting a knight on a bier with the Latin text 'HIC DEFVNCIVS EST' (Here he is defeated), as the narrator solemnly declares that William was 'at last faced by death, the greatest foe of all.' A deep, resonant bell tolls as the camera zooms in on the tapestry knight's embroidered face, coinciding with the narration of the 'great bell of the Minster of Rouen' tolling on September 9th, 1087, when 'the fierce old warrior breathed his last.' The somber mood continues with a shift to a dim, candlelit cave, where flickering flames cast dancing shadows on stone walls, creating a mysterious atmosphere. Finally, the video cuts to a falconer warrior in a studded leather vest with red sleeves, holding a falcon on his gloved hand in a wooded area with bare trees, suggesting a moment of calm or contemplation, as the narrator concludes with a monk's account of the conqueror's tormented words of guilt and anguish, serving as his epitaph. Throughout, a somber, historical orchestral score underscores the narrative.

The segment opens with a close-up of a Falconer Warrior in a studded leather vest with red sleeves, holding a falcon in a wooded area, as a deep, solemn male voice, identified as William, begins a reflective monologue: "I persecuted the native inhabitants of England beyond all reason, whether nobles or commons, I cruelly oppressed them." The scene transitions to a misty, overcast battlefield, depicting Armored Warriors in chainmail engaged in a tense battle reenactment amidst smoke and fire, suggesting a village under siege, while William's voice continues, "Many I unjustly disinherited, innumerable multitudes, especially in the county of York." Warriors then appear on a Rocky Outcrop, silhouetted against a pale sky, one holding a sword aloft, followed by a brief stylized illustration of a yellow flag with a green serpent. A Warrior in Chainmail stands solemnly, adjusting his gauntlets, his expression resolute, as William's narration reflects, "Perished through me by famine and sword. I tremble when I reflect on the grievous sins which burden my conscience." The video then cuts to a black screen with blue text revealing the title 'William The Conqueror' and production credits. William's voice continues his somber reflection, "And now, about to be summoned before the most awful tribunal of God, I know not what I ought to do. I was bred to arms from my childhood, and am stained with the rivers of blood that I have shed." As the credits continue to roll, William's monologue concludes, and dramatic, percussive, and string-heavy instrumental music with a distinct Middle Eastern or folk influence, featuring a prominent string instrument and hand drums, begins to build intensity, accompanying the remaining production team and historical expert credits.

The video segment opens with rolling production credits displayed in glowing blue text against a black background, listing key personnel including Production Manager Gareth Blyth, Expert Analysis contributors Dr. David Chandler, John Barratt, and Matthew Strickland, Editor Chris Gormlie, Executive Producers Bob Carruthers, Geoff Kempin, and Michael Lopez, and Director Michael Leighton. The credits conclude with 'Produced and Directed by Jeremy Freeston' and the copyright notice '©Cromwell Productions 1996'. Throughout the display of these credits, a dramatic and melancholic orchestral score plays, featuring strings and possibly brass, creating a formal and reflective atmosphere. As the music fades out around the 0:15 mark, the Mercury Studios logo appears – a stylized silver profile of a winged helmet with the word 'Mercury' in cursive script and 'STUDIOS' beneath it. This logo then fades, leaving a dark, textured gray screen that remains static and silent for the remainder of the segment, marking a clear transition or conclusion.
